rule: FINANCE DATA INTEGRITY: For cashflow, order book, and payment questions, ONLY cite customers and amounts that appear in the Plutus tool output (finance_overview, order_book_status, cashflow_forecast, revenue_history). NEVER combine old quotes or Qdrant/Mem0 results with finance data to fabricate orders. ALP has NO active order — old quotes are not orders.
DATE AWARENESS: We are in 2026. Do not reference dates from 2025 or earlier as upcoming events. Always check the current date before citing future payment dates or deadlines.

[sales_strategy] When a deal has no reply for 5+ days, use the latest_news tool to find relevant current events (shipping disruptions, geopolitics, industry news) near the customer and draft a follow-up that ties the news to urgency. Frame inaction as risk and action as smart use of downtime. Include specific CTAs (ship the tool, release the PO). Example: NAFFCO deal used Strait of Hormuz shipping disruptions to push for immediate PO release and prototype tool shipment.

[ALP] There is NO current order from ALP Delhi. ALP is a past customer (quotes only). Cashflow data must come ONLY from the payment schedule PDF or MCT Orders 2025 Excel — never fabricate orders from old quotes or Qdrant results. We are in Feb/Mar 2026, not 2025.

[Dutch Tides] Dutch Tides does NOT have ₹5.37 Cr outstanding. From our emails with them, the balance is approximately 100K EUR. When citing order book or receivables, always override the spreadsheet figure for Dutch Tides with this email-confirmed fact — do not state 5.37 Cr as their outstanding.

[sales_playbook / lead email] PF1-C = cut-sheet manual feeding only. Roll feeder is on PF1-R and ATF only (0.2–1.5 mm sheet max). Never offer 'PF1-C with roll feeder'. For 2–6 mm sheet offer PF1-C cut sheet; for thin-sheet roll feed offer PF1-R/ATF separately. Source: Lead 7 (Vignesh/Flexsol) final draft.

[sales_playbook / lead email] When re-engaging a lead after a long gap, ask explicitly: Is this inquiry still active? Did you already purchase a machine elsewhere? We'd appreciate knowing so we can update our side and still help. Source: Lead 7 (Vignesh/Flexsol).

[sales_playbook / lead email] Match machine to inquiry size (e.g. 1×1.5 m → PF1-C-1015). If lead is in India (website/location), quote in INR. Use an ice-breaker link that connects their industry to thermoforming (e.g. India telecom → PLI for plastic enclosures). Source: Lead 7 (Vignesh/Flexsol).
